prs.title: [ROCm] Use quant_dtype in per_token_quant instead of hardcoded FP8
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
`_rocm_aiter_per_token_quant_impl` and its fake both accept `quant_dtype` (which can be `torch.int8` or `FP8_DTYPE` per the assert), but hardcode `FP8_DTYPE` for the output tensor allocation. This means passing `torch.int8` would still get an FP8 tensor back.

Simple fix: use `quant_dtype` instead of `FP8_DTYPE` for `torch.empty()`.

Currently all callers pass `FP8_DTYPE` so this hasn't surfaced, but it's a correctness bug if int8 quantization is ever enabled on this path.

No duplicate PRs found (PR #36092 covers other _aiter_ops bugs but not this one). AI assistance was used for code search; all changes reviewed by human submitter.

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortstheboat@gmail.com>

prs.title: [Attention Backend] TurboQuant: 2-bit KV cache compression with 4x capacity
prs.author: vibhavagarwal5
prs.body_head:
### Summary

TurboQuant adds **online KV cache compression** to vLLM's v1 attention backend using PolarQuant (WHT rotation + Lloyd-Max scalar quantization) for keys and uniform quantization for values. All quantization happens at store time via fused Triton kernels — no offline calibration, model changes, or weight modifications required. Just set `--kv-cache-dtype turboquant_k8v4`.

### Compression Presets (Qwen3-4B, head_dim=128)

| Preset | Key | Value | Slot (bytes) | Compression | GSM8K | NIAH |
|--------|-----|-------|-------------|-------------|-------|------|
| `turboquant_k8v4` | FP8 (E4M3) | 4-bit uniform | 196 | **2.6x** | 0.860 | 100% |
| `turboquant_4bit_nc` | 4-bit MSE + NC | 4-bit uniform + NC | 136 | **3.8x** | 0.840 | 100% |
| `turboquant_k3v4_nc` | 3-bit MSE + NC | 4-bit uniform + NC | 120 | **4.3x** | 0.780 | 100% |
| `turboquant_3bit_nc` | 3-bit MSE + NC | 3-bit uniform + NC | 104 | **4.9x** | 0.720 | 100% |

Baseline: GSM8K 0.900, NIAH 100%. Measured on Qwen/Qwen3-4B with 5-shot GSM8K (200q) and NIAH (512-32K, 77 probes).

### Performance (Qwen3-4B, 4x RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell, cudagraphs+compile)

#### Throughput (output tok/s)

| Scenario | Baseline | k8v4 | % base | t4nc | % base | k3v4nc | % base | t3nc | % base |
|----------|---------|------|--------|------|--------|--------|--------|------|--------|
| short-decode (128→512) | 8977 | **7113** | **79%** | 6397 | 71% | 6206 | 69% | 6114 | 68% |
| long-prefill (4096→128) | 850 | **811** | **95%** | 766 | 90% | 745 | 88% | 730 | 86% |
| mixed (512→512) | 6618 | **5279** | **80%** | 4829 | 73% | 4584 | 69% | 4491 | 68% |
| high-load (512→128, n=500) | 5633 | **4751** | **84%** | 4456 | 79% | 4337 | 77% | 4240 | 75% |
| very-long-prefill (8192→64) | 233 | **234** | **100%** | 224 | 96% | 220 | 94% | 216 | 93% |
| decode-heavy (64→1024) | 8304 | **6521** | **79%** | 5887 | 71% | 5650 | 68% | 5430 | 65% |

#### TPOT (ms) — lower is better

| Scenario | baseline | k8v4 |

prs.title: [WIP] Add TRITON_MLA_SPARSE backend for SM80 sparse MLA support
prs.author: haosdent
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Closes #38006. Enables sparse MLA models (GLM-5, DeepSeek-V3.2) on SM80 (A100/A800) and SM121 (GB10/DGX Spark), where DeepGEMM / FlashMLA-Sparse / FlashInfer-MLA-Sparse are unavailable.

Three changes:

1. **Guard fix.** `has_deep_gemm()` (package check) → `is_deep_gemm_supported()` (SM90+ arch check) in `sparse_attn_indexer.py` and `indexer.py`. Prevents DeepGEMM SM90+ kernels from being invoked on SM80/SM121 when the Python package happens to be installed.

2. **Triton indexer MQA logits.** `mqa_logits_triton.py` reproduces DeepGEMM's `fp8_mqa_logits` / `fp8_paged_mqa_logits` (bf16 tensor-core matmul with fp32 accumulator + scale, ReLU + per-head weighting, sum across heads). PyTorch reference stays in `mqa_logits_fallback.py`. On GB10, `fp8_paged_mqa_logits` at ctx=2048 goes from 6.4 ms (torch loop) → 0.03 ms (~250×), saving ~380 ms/token across 60 sparse layers.

3. **New `TRITON_MLA_SPARSE` backend** (`triton_mla_sparse.py` → `triton_sparse_mla_kernel.py`). Single pure-Triton kernel with split-KV for low-batch decode (`num_kv_splits > 1` when the single-pass grid under-fills the device) and a single-pass fast path otherwise. Stage 2 merges per-split `(out, lse)` tiles via online-softmax rescaling (pattern from `triton_decode_attention.py`). `@triton.autotune` over `(num_warps, num_stages)` × `(index_topk, NUM_KV_SPLITS)`; warmed in `TritonMLASparseImpl.__init__` so the first user request does not pay the sweep cost. Registered in `AttentionBackendEnum` and added to the non-SM100 MLA priority list.

**Kernel benchmark on NVIDIA A30 (SM80, 56 SMs):**

| shape | XPU kernel | this PR | speedup |
|---|---|---|---|
| nt=1, topk=2048, H=128 (decode) | 0.297 ms | 0.094 ms | **3.15×** |
| nt=1, topk=4096, H=128 | 0.580 ms | 0.167 ms | 3.46× |
| nt=1, topk=4096, H=16 | 0.582 ms | 0.081 ms | 7.17× |
| nt=128, topk=2048, H=128 (prefill) | 4.912 ms | 4.872 ms | 1.01× |

Decode (nt=1) across all (topk, H): median **4.30×**, best 7.17×. Non-decode (nt ≥ 8): autotu

prs.title: [ROCm] Implement GPU-to-NUMA-node detection
prs.author: pschlan-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Implement automatic NUMA topology detection on ROCm platforms via the amdsmi library (which is already a dependency).

## Test Plan
Run vLLM with `--numa-bind`, observe correct topology detection

## Test Result
Topology is detected correctly on the test system with 8 GPUs connected to 2 NUMA nodes:
```INFO 04-16 12:57:41 [numa_utils.py:111] Auto-detected NUMA nodes for GPUs: [0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1]```

prs.title: [ROCm][Engine] Fix GPU memory leaks in engine shutdown and test workaround for async KV prefix cache reset
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
When running in-process (`VLLM_ENABLE_V1_MULTIPROCESSING=0`), shutting down the engine leaked GPU memory because:

- **`supports_kw` LRU cache pinned model weights**: The `@lru_cache` on `supports_kw` was keyed on bound methods (e.g. `model.forward`), which held strong references to the model instance and all its GPU tensors for the lifetime of the cache. Fixed by unwrapping bound methods to `__func__` before caching.
- **`gc.freeze()` was never undone**: `EngineCore.__init__` calls `gc.freeze()` to reduce GC overhead during serving, but `shutdown()` never called `gc.unfreeze()`, making all engine objects invisible to the garbage collector.
- **No explicit GPU resource teardown**: `GPUWorker.shutdown()` never freed model weights, KV caches, the rotary embedding cache (`_ROPE_DICT`), compilation contexts, or the workspace singleton.

### Test workaround for prefix cache reset

`reset_prefix_cache` could return `False` indefinitely when async KV store transfers (CPU offloading) were in flight, because the engine stops stepping after all requests finish and never polls the worker for completed transfers. As a temporary workaround (per reviewer suggestion), `_wait_for_prefix_cache_reset` in the test now sends dummy single-token prefills between retries to force the engine to step, which naturally drains completed transfers and frees GPU blocks.

### UCX fix

Bump UCX branch in ROCm Dockerfile to `33b3b2a2` which fixes a memory leak in UCX when used with NIXL.

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest tests/v1/kv_offload/ tests/v1/kv_connector/unit/ tests/v1/core/test_scheduler.py tests/v1/core/test_prefix_caching.py tests/v1/core/test_reset_prefix_cache_e2e.py tests/v1/simple_kv_offload/test_integration.py -v`

### [LEGACY] Initial Test plan and Motivation

- [x] `pytest tests/v1/kv_offload/test_cpu_offloading.py::test_cpu_offloading[TRITON_ATTN-48]`

Motivation: https://buildkite.com/vllm/amd-ci/builds/7062/steps/canvas?sid=019d382d-caab-452a-bf83-1c611ef498b6

prs.title: [ROCm] Add gfx1102/gfx1103 support
prs.author: mgehre-amd
prs.body_head:
gfx1103 (RDNA 3, e.g. Radeon 780M iGPU) was missing from the HIP_SUPPORTED_ARCHS list in CMakeLists.txt and from the compile-time architecture macros in skinny_gemms.cu and attention.cu.

The skinny GEMM and attention kernels defined __HIP__GFX1X__ and __HIP__GFX11__ by listing individual arch targets (__gfx1100__, __gfx1101__, __gfx1150__, __gfx1151__) but missed __gfx1103__. This caused wvSplitK kernel bodies to compile as UNREACHABLE_CODE (assert false) on gfx1103 while the wave32 reduction code was still active, resulting in a crash at runtime.

Replace __HIP__GFX11__ / __HIP__GFX12__ with the compiler-provided __GFX11__ / __GFX12__ umbrella macros which are defined for every target in each family. This avoids needing to enumerate individual targets and automatically covers future gfx11xx/gfx12xx additions.

prs.title: `[Perf][MoE][ROCm][Kimi-K2.5] Remove a redundant per-forward-pass dtype conversion of the routing bias parameter in DeepSeek-V2/V3 MoE`
prs.author: xaguilar-amd
prs.body_head: ## Purpose Fixes a redundant per-forward-pass dtype conversion of the routing bias parameter (`e_score_correction_bias`) in the DeepSeek-V2 / DeepSeek-V3 / Kimi-K2 family of MoE models. ### Root cause In `vllm/model_executor/models/deepseek_v2.py`, the routing bias is allocated in `torch.float32`: ```python self.gate.e_score_correction_bias = nn.Parameter( torch.empty(config.n_routed_experts, dtype=torch.float32) ) ``` However the router GEMM (`GateLinear`) produces `gating_output` in a dtype chosen later via `self.gate.set_out_dtype(...)`: - `float32` for monolithic + DeepSeekV3 routing - `bfloat16` for all other paths (DeepSeekV2, Kimi-K2.5, Mistral Large 3, etc.) On the ROCm aiter fused-MoE path, `vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/rocm_aiter_fused_moe.py` then forces the bias to match the gating output dtype every call: ```python # rocm_aiter_fused_moe.py:152-162 if e_score_correction_bias is not None: rocm_aiter_ops.biased_grouped_topk( gating_output, e_score_correction_bias.to(gating_output.dtype), # <-- launches fp32 -> bf16 copy every step topk_weights, topk_ids, ... ) ``` Because `e_score_correction_bias` is a small, constant parameter that never changes after weight loading, doing this conversion once — at construction time — is sufficient. Today it is instead launched as a separate kernel between the gate GEMM and the `biased_grouped_topk` kernel on every MoE layer, on every decode step. ### Impact in a Perfetto trace Profiled on Kimi-K2.5-MXFP4 (ROCm): Before After ### Why fix it in `DeepseekV2MoE` rather than in `rocm_aiter_fused_moe.py` The `.to(gating

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Add missing quantization methods and fix online quant test failures
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Several quantization methods were failing on ROCm because they weren't registered in the platform's `supported_quantization` list, even though the underlying kernels already support them (either natively or through emulation fallbacks).

This adds `fp8_per_tensor`, `fp8_per_block`, `online`, `gpt_oss_mxfp4`, `mxfp8`, `modelopt`, `modelopt_mxfp8`, and `modelopt_mixed` to ROCm's supported quantization allowlist. These all have working codepaths on ROCm. FP8 variants use `ROCmFP8ScaledMMLinearKernel`, mxfp8 and modelopt_mxfp8 use `EmulationMxfp8LinearKernel` (introduced in #39205), and the online quant schemes resolve to fp8 methods that already work.

Also fixes two issues in the test infrastructure that were blocking the online quantization peak memory test on ROCm:

1. The peak GPU memory log in `base_loader.py` was gated on `is_cuda()` only, so it never fired on ROCm. Changed to `is_cuda_alike()` which covers both CUDA and ROCm since `torch.accelerator.max_memory_allocated()` works on both.

2. The memory thresholds in `test_online_quant_peak_mem` were hardcoded for CUDA's `fp8_e4m3fn`. ROCm on gfx950 uses `fp8_e4m3fnuz` which results in slightly higher memory usage (~7.45 GiB vs ~6.65 GiB), so the thresholds are now platform-aware.

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm][Perf] Replace WNA16 MoE Triton kernel with FlyDSL MoE
prs.author: amd-asalykov
prs.body_head: ## Purpose This PR replaces WNA16 MoE GPTQ Triton kernel with FlyDSL MoE kernel if 1. weights are 4-bit quantized 2. group_size==-1 or 32 targeting Kimi K2.5 INT4 model ## Test Plan Measure e2e perf. and test accuracy on gsm8k ## Test Result Accuracy: ~0.94 Perf: ---

prs.title: [Bugfix] Fix scaled_mm output narrowing for 3D input tensors
prs.author: nemanjaudovic
prs.body_head:
When the input activation x is 3D (e.g. [batch, seq, hidden_in]), the base class flattens it to 2D before calling apply_scaled_mm, but passes the original 3D shape as
  output_shape. The narrowing step then used output_shape[0] (batch only) instead of the full number of tokens (which for 3D inputs is output_shape[0] * output_shape[1], or in 
  general math.prod(output_shape[:-1])). Fix by using math.prod(output_shape[:-1]) in all three pytorch kernel variants so the correct number of rows is used regardless of    
  input rank.                                                                                                                                                                   
                  
  Co-authored-by: Claude
                                                                                                                                                                                
  Purpose                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                
  Fix incorrect output narrowing in pytorch FP8 scaled_mm kernels when input activations are 3D tensors. When initializing the Phi model with:                                  
```python                  
  from vllm import LLM                                                                                                                                                          
  llm = LLM(                                                                                                                                                                    
      model='nm-testing/Phi-3.5-vision-instruct-FP8-dynamic',
